steps.title: Identify the Boyne's ranking
steps.content: The passage explicitly states that only the Britannia was ranked first-rate. This means the Boyne was not first-rate, so it must be lower-ranked.

steps.title: Apply the crew-size rule
steps.content:
Lower-ranked ships have fewer crew than first-rate ships (which had between 850 and 875), so the Boyne had fewer than 850.

With three decks, some three-deck ships therefore had fewer than 850 crew.

Choices B, C, and D don't work:
  • B speculates about operational needs beyond the passage
  • C reverses the crew hierarchy (Boyne had fewer crew than Britannia)
  • D is contradicted by Boyne's 98 cannons and fewer-than-850 crew
.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
